name: Puertos de Torremolinos Trail
name_original: Pico Palomas – circular desde Torremolinos por el Tajo de las Palomas
description: This difficult 5.3-mile jogging route in Montes de Torremolinos offers panoramic views and a challenging 1635 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Torremolinos traverse a varied landscape, from extensive coastal promenades to mountainous areas and pine forests. The region features flat, scenic paths along the Mediterranean coast, as well as more challenging trails with significant elevation gains in its interior. Urban routes also provide opportunities to explore the town's central areas. Best jogging routes around Torremolinos The most popular jogging route is Puertos de Torremolinos Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging elevation and panoramic coastal views. Another top favourite among local runners is Puertos de Torremolinos Trail, a difficult 5.2 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail leads through mountainous terrain, providing extensive views of the Costa del Sol. Local runners also love the Beach – El Sardiná Beach Bar loop from Torremolinos, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 57 minutes. Jogging around Torremolinos is defined by coastal promenades, pine forests, and mountain trails. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Torremolinos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 10,000 runners have used komoot to explore Torremolinos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I find in Torremolinos?
A: Torremolinos offers a diverse range of running experiences. You can enjoy flat, scenic runs along the Mediterranean coast on its extensive promenades, explore urban routes through the town's central areas, or challenge yourself on mountain and forest trails in the interior. This variety caters to all fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running paths in Torremolinos?
A: Yes, Torremolinos has several easy running options. The coastal promenades, such as those along Playa de la Carihuela or from Los Álamos to Saltillo, offer flat and accessible paths perfect for beginners or a relaxed jog. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33976708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach – El Sardiná Beach Bar loop from Torremolinos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with minimal elevation gain, suitable for many runners.

Q: What are some scenic spots or natural features to look out for on a run in Torremolinos?
A: Torremolinos boasts several beautiful natural features. The &lt;strong&gt;Cañada del Lobo&lt;/strong&gt; (Wolf's Ravine) offers trails with stunning viewpoints of the Costa del Sol. The &lt;strong&gt;Pinares de Torremolinos&lt;/strong&gt; provides a refreshing pine forest environment. For coastal views, the seafront promenade and sections of the &lt;strong&gt;Coastal Path (Senda Litoral)&lt;/strong&gt; are ideal. You can also find green spaces like &lt;strong&gt;Parque de la Batería&lt;/strong&gt;, which features a watchtower with sea views.

Q: Are there any running routes that are suitable for families with children?
A: For family-friendly running, the flat and well-maintained promenades along the beaches are excellent choices. &lt;strong&gt;Parque de la Batería&lt;/strong&gt; is another great option, offering green spaces, paths, and a jogging track within an urban park setting, making it safe and enjoyable for families. While not specifically a running route, the &lt;strong&gt;Molino de Inca Botanical Garden&lt;/strong&gt; offers a tranquil environment for a leisurely walk or light jog.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Torremolinos?
A: Many outdoor areas in Torremolinos are dog-friendly, especially the natural trails in areas like &lt;strong&gt;Cañada del Lobo&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Pinares de Torremolinos&lt;/strong&gt;. When running with your dog, always ensure they are on a leash and that you clean up after them. For specific regulations on promenades or urban parks, it's advisable to check local signage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Torremolinos?
A: Torremolinos is excellent for running year-round due to its pleasant climate, with over 300 sunny days and an average temperature of 20°C. Spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable conditions. Even in summer, early mornings or late evenings provide cooler temperatures for coastal runs. Winters are mild, making it a great destination for escaping colder climates.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Torremolinos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33976708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach – El Sardiná Beach Bar loop from Torremolinos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31552823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galá Statue – Beach loop from El Pinillo&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that offer varied scenery.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trails?
A: Parking is generally available in Torremolinos. For coastal runs, you can often find street parking or public car parks near the promenades. For mountain and forest trails, such as those in Cañada del Lobo or Pinares de Torremolinos, designated parking areas are usually available at trailheads or nearby access points. It's always a good idea to check specific route details for parking recommendations.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest I can see while running?
A: While running, you might encounter various points of interest. The &lt;strong&gt;Parque de la Batería&lt;/strong&gt; features a historic watchtower. For those interested in broader historical sites, the region around Torremolinos offers attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7262649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Alcazaba of Málaga and the Roman Theatre&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/90115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomares Castle&lt;/a&gt;, though these might require a slight detour from direct running paths.

Q: How many running routes are there in Torremolinos?
A: There are over 60 dedicated running routes around Torremolinos available on komoot. These routes range in difficulty from easy to difficult, ensuring options for every runner.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Torremolinos?
A: The running routes in Torremolinos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 90 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscape, from scenic coastal paths to challenging mountain trails, and the opportunity to enjoy the pleasant climate and beautiful views year-round.

Q: Are there any longer distance running options for experienced runners?
A: Yes, experienced runners looking for a challenge can explore routes with significant elevation gains in the mountainous areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996214250/puertos-de-torremolinos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Torremolinos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 8.5 km route with challenging elevation and panoramic coastal views. Additionally, Torremolinos is part of the larger &lt;strong&gt;Gran Senda de Málaga&lt;/strong&gt;, offering extensive routes for ambitious runners.
tips: Beautiful and lonely little valley above Torremolinos.

faqs.question: Is the Puertos de Torremolinos Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as difficult and is best suited for joggers with good physical fitness. It features significant ascents and descents, with narrow, rocky, and sometimes slippery terrain, particularly on the final descent. It's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is challenging, characterized by narrow, rocky paths and considerable ascents and descents. Some sections can be dangerous and slippery, especially where they coincide with mountain bike circuits. Good physical fitness and sure-footedness are essential.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking panoramic views throughout the route. Looking south, you'll see the Bay of Malaga and the Costa del Sol, while to the north, the Guadalhorce Valley comes into view. The trail also offers spectacular vistas of the surrounding mountains and the town of Torremolinos below.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes several notable points. You'll encounter the stunning Tajo de las Palomas and reach the Palomas Peak. Another highlight is the Cañada del Lobo viewpoint, offering some of the finest views of the Costa del Sol, marked by a striking sculpture of a howling wolf. The route also passes by the Cueva del Toro (Bull's Cave) and an ancient acequia (water channel) from the Cañuelo spring.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this trail is during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers in Torremolinos can be very hot, making the challenging ascents and descents more difficult and potentially unsafe. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Puertos de Torremolinos Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the trail typically starts from Torremolinos. It's advisable to research local parking options in Torremolinos that offer convenient access to the Montes de Torremolinos area, or consider public transport to the town center and then a short walk to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Montes de Torremolinos?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for accessing the natural areas of the Montes de Torremolinos for recreational activities like jogging. However, always respect local regulations and signage, especially concerning protected areas or private land.

faqs.question: Is the Puertos de Torremolinos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many natural trails in the region are dog-friendly, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash due to the presence of wildlife and potentially dangerous terrain. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a difficult route with significant elevation changes and rocky sections.

faqs.question: Is navigation straightforward on the trail?
faqs.answer: Navigation can be challenging in some areas due to poor signage. It is highly recommended to carry a reliable map or use a GPS device with the trail loaded to ensure you stay on course. The route is also known as PR-A 172.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers a significant portion of the official 'Puertos de Torremolinos' trail (PR-A 172). It also passes through smaller sections of the 'Arroyo Zambrano - Pico Jabalcuza' and 'Benalmádena - Alhaurín de la Torre' trails, connecting it to the larger Gran Senda de Málaga (Great Malaga Path) network.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through areas rich in Mediterranean flora and fauna. You might spot wildlife such as squirrels, mongooses, and hedgehogs. Overhead, keep an eye out for birds of prey like eagles and kestrels, as well as partridges. Be aware that vipers can also be present in the area.
